The provided global filter only excludes rows where both exam_score and project_score are NULL.","Visualization expects an unpivoted result (one row per department × metric). The SQL should produce columns: department_name (departments.name), metric (literal 'Exam' or 'Project'), avg_score (the corresponding average).","All semesters/years and all courses are included unless further filters are requested (no restriction to active enrollments or specific academic_year/semester).","Scores are treated equally (not weighted by course credits or enrollment size beyond the natural averaging).","departments.name is used as the department label; if departments.name is not sufficiently unique, department.id or departments.code could be used instead upon request."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"WITH dept_avgs AS (
  SELECT d.name AS department_name,
         AVG(g.exam_score) AS avg_exam_score,
         AVG(g.project_score) AS avg_project_score
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY d.name
)
SELECT department_name, 'Exam' AS metric, avg_exam_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
UNION ALL
SELECT department_name, 'Project' AS metric, avg_project_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
ORDER BY department_name ASC, metric ASC;","validationIssues":["Some departments may have NULL for one metric if no exam or project scores exist there; the query leaves avg_score NULL for that metric so the bar will be absent—consider COALESCE or filtering if you want explicit zeros.","The y-axis scale is fixed to domain [0,100]; if your score range differs this could clip or underrepresent the data—consider using an automatic domain or confirming the 0–100 assumption.","Long department names may overlap despite labelAngle -40; consider truncation or wrapping if readability is an issue.","Averages are unweighted (simple AVG). If you intended to weight by enrollment size or course credit, the SQL would need to change—current behavior matches the stated assumptions."]},"historySummary":"Attempt 1: passed","latest":{"sqlDraft":{"reasoning":"Aggregates exam and project averages per department using confirmed join path, then unpivots results to one row per department × metric.   FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL

)
SELECT department_name, 'Exam' AS metric, avg_exam_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
UNION ALL
SELECT department_name, 'Project' AS metric, avg_project_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
ORDER BY department_name ASC, metric ASC;","assumptions":["All referenced tables exist in schema public (confirmed).","Join path confirmed: grades.enrollment_id -> enrollments.id -> enrollments.course_section_id -> course_sections.id -> course_sections.course_id -> courses.id -> courses.department_id -> departments.id.","grades.enrollment_id is unique (one grades row per enrollment) so no additional deduplication was needed.","AVG() ignores NULLs, so per-metric averages will be NULL if no non-NULL values exist for that metric in a department."]},"execution":{"ok":true,"sql":"WITH dept_avgs AS (

data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach
Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.title: Average Exam vs Project Scores by Department

resultRaw.sql:
WITH dept_avgs AS (
  SELECT d.name AS department_name,
         AVG(g.exam_score) AS avg_exam_score,
         AVG(g.project_score) AS avg_project_score
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY d.name
)
SELECT department_name, 'Exam' AS metric, avg_exam_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
UNION ALL
SELECT department_name, 'Project' AS metric, avg_project_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
ORDER BY department_name ASC, metric ASC;

issues: Some departments may have NULL for one metric if no exam or project scores exist there; the query leaves avg_score NULL for that metric so the bar will be absent—consider COALESCE or filtering if you want explicit zeros.
The y-axis scale is fixed to domain [0,100]; if your score range differs this could clip or underrepresent the data—consider using an automatic domain or confirming the 0–100 assumption.
Long department names may overlap despite labelAngle -40; consider truncation or wrapping if readability is an issue.
Averages are unweighted (simple AVG). If you intended to weight by enrollment size or course credit, the SQL would need to change—current behavior matches the stated assumptions.

visualization.title: Average Exam vs Project Scores by Department

result.sql:
WITH dept_avgs AS (
  SELECT d.name AS department_name,
         AVG(g.exam_score) AS avg_exam_score,
         AVG(g.project_score) AS avg_project_score
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY d.name
)
SELECT department_name, 'Exam' AS metric, avg_exam_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
UNION ALL
SELECT department_name, 'Project' AS metric, avg_project_score AS avg_score
FROM dept_avgs
ORDER BY department_name ASC, metric ASC;

issues: Some departments may have NULL for one metric if no exam or project scores exist there; the query leaves avg_score NULL for that metric so the bar will be absent—consider COALESCE or filtering if you want explicit zeros.
The y-axis scale is fixed to domain [0,100]; if your score range differs this could clip or underrepresent the data—consider using an automatic domain or confirming the 0–100 assumption.
Long department names may overlap despite labelAngle -40; consider truncation or wrapping if readability is an issue.
Averages are unweighted (simple AVG). If you intended to weight by enrollment size or course credit, the SQL would need to change—current behavior matches the stated assumptions.
